channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Cortez Masto leads Nevada Democrats in renewed effort against nuclear waste dump
item.description: Nevada's Democratic U.S. senators and representatives in Congress reintroduced the Nuclear Waste Informed Consent Act on Wednesday, gearing up for possible renewed efforts to put a nuclear dump in the state.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Five things to know about Biden's farewell address that also served as a warning to the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With only days left in the White House, President Joe Biden was saving a few surprises for his farewell address Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's outsider Cabinet picks are rapidly gaining support for confirmation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One by one, all the president-elect’ s men, and women, are falling into place in his Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson removes chair of powerful House Intelligence Committee
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is removing the GOP chairman of the powerful House Intelligence Committee, who was a vocal supporter of assistance for Ukraine and held other views that put him at odds with President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate adds amendment to immigration bill named after Laken Riley
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday adopted the first amendment to the Laken Riley Act, as Republicans push for a legislative win to open the new Congress. The legislation in its current form would mandate federal detention of immigrants without legal status accused of theft, burglary and other related crimes.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ratcliffe edges closer to CIA confirmation with Democratic support: 5 takeaways
item.description: John Ratcliffe emerged largely unscathed from a hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee that appeared to pave the way for him to serve as the next director of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Supreme Court weighs postponing election crucial to Capitol control
item.description: Minnesota Supreme Court justices on Wednesday promised to decide quickly whether a special Roseville-area election critical to deciding power in the House happens as scheduled on Jan. 28 or gets pushed deeper into the legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis hopes Trump will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: "There's been no issue that has been higher on the radar," DeSantis added. "We need all hands on deck.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson removes Mike Turner as Intelligence Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) will not make Rep. Mike Turner (R-Ohio) chair of the House Intelligence Committee in the 119th Congress, a source familiar with the decision confirmed to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Biden both claim credit for Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and President-elect Donald Trump are both claiming credit for Israel and Hamas agreeing to a ceasefire deal in Gaza on Wednesday after the White House brought Trump's Middle East envoy into negotiations that have dragged on for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, AAMER MADHANI and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada attorney general moves to protect health care access for DACA recipients
item.description: Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford is intervening to protect access to health care insurance for Dreamers — the recipients of the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) policy.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York to open massive new 2,200-bed migrant shelter for single men in the Bronx
item.description: After New York Mayor Eric Adams announced plans for a 2,000-bed migrant shelter in the Bronx, Rep. Ritchie Torres slammed the decision, accusing Adams of creating an immigrant “dumping ground.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flags ordered to fly at full-staff in multiple states on Inauguration Day
item.description: The 30-day mourning period, reserved for the death of a sitting or former president, would have overlapped with the inauguration of President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pick for budget director won't say whether he would release Ukraine aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russell Vought, President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for White House budget director, declined Wednesday to fully commit to doling out congressionally approved funds, specifically U.S. military aid to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the special election for Vargas' Board of Supervisors seat
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors on Tuesday finalized plans to fill the seat formerly held by Nora Vargas before her abrupt exit from elected office through a special election.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how to watch Biden's farewell address from the Oval Office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday night, a speech designed to highlight his administration's accomplishments over the past four years.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Just 2 in 10 confident Trump DOJ will act fairly: Survey
item.description: GOP voters are divided when it comes to their faith in the DOJ being neutral even with the incoming Republican administration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Confirmation delays stack up for Trump nominees as paperwork lags in federal offices
item.description: Paperwork delays have forced some Trump confirmation hearings to be postponed, with Republicans blaming bureacracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thompson, chair of Jan. 6 committee, has talked with White House about pardons
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.), who headed Congress’s special investigation into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, said Tuesday that he has been in talks with the White House about presidential pardons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio vows to place US interests 'above all else' as Trump's top diplomat
item.description: Florida Sen. Marco Rubio promised on Wednesday to implement President-elect Donald Trump’s “America First” vision as secretary of state, vowing in his confirmation hearing that the incoming administration will forge a new path by placing American interests “above all else.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATTHEW LEE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota legislative standoff spills out of the Statehouse into the courthouse
item.description: With accusations flying over which party is ignoring state law, the fight to control the Minnesota House is shifting from lawmakers to lawyers. DFL Secretary of State Steve Simon is calling on the Minnesota Supreme Court to weigh in on who has what power.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Departing DHS Secretary Mayorkas contends he delivered border security in the end
item.description: As DHS Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas prepares to leave office, NPR sits down for an exit interview. He tells us the border is more secure now than before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara and Steve Inskeep, MPR News

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: Joe Biden staked his presidency on the idea that he was the best person to keep Donald Trump out of power.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: These 2 Democrats voted for House Republicans' bill to bar transgender athletes from girls sports
item.description: Reps. Vicente Gonzalez and Henry Cuellar, both of Texas, were the only Democrats to side with Republicans on Tuesday to pass legislation meant to bar transgender athletes from competing in girls and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth confronts allegations of misconduct as senators grill Trump’s choice for Pentagon chief
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth was combative at times, as he was forced to confront the allegations of misconduct and his own comments that are far from the military mainstream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Live updates: Democrats turn up heat on Hegseth as Republican alleges hypocrisy
item.description: Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday, the first of President-elect Trump's Cabinet candidates to face confirmation.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump's Greenland demands could impact Ozempic costs
item.description: President-elect Trump's threat to tariff Denmark if it resists his acquisition plans for the island territory of Greenland could disrupt one export that is wildly popular in America: Ozempic.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: What Project 2025 says about the first 180 days of a presidency
item.description: Project 2025 lists four pillars as part of its plan, and the fourth pillar is an unpublished 180-day "Transition Playbook.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis's call for special session 'premature,' Senate President, House Speaker say
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Exit interview: DOT Sec. Buttigieg on infrastructure act and the road ahead
item.description: In an exit interview with All Things Considered, DOT Secretary Pete Buttigieg reflects on the Biden administration's infrastructure act and why it didn't resonate with some voters.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow, Avery Keatley, and Adam Raney, MPR News

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5
